title: chamaesyce buxifolia
intro: Chamaesyce buxifolia, also known as boxwood spurge or boxwood sandmat, is a succulent plant species in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae) that is native to the Americas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chamaesyce buxifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
